sub_problems: How does an agent runtime detect which prior claims a downstream step actually relied on, so the dependency graph between claims and uses can be made explicit during long trajectories?

Once reliance has been recorded, how does the runtime lock a claim against silent retraction, requiring instead an explicit retraction-with-cascade-recovery operation when a prior claim must be reversed?
How can claim-retraction events be priced into evaluation — what is the appropriate cost function over load-bearing-claim contradictions, distinct from end-task success metrics?

How should the runtime handle the equivalent of judicial estoppel — preventing the agent from taking position A in one branch of a multi-branch task and contradictory position not-A in another branch when both are returned to the user?
